alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP methods were developed to automatically detect tweets by users reporting their birth defect outcomes.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same contribution (automated detection of birth-defect-reporting tweets) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research included a comparison of feature-engineered and deep learning-based classifiers trained on imbalanced, under-sampled, and over-sampled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An SVM classifier outperformed a deep neural network-based classifier for highly imbalanced social media data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint describes a broad set of comparisons (including sampling strategies); the paper emphasizes the primary comparative outcome (SVM vs DNN) for imbalanced data, narrowing the methodological emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An error analysis was conducted that could inform classification improvements using the publicly available corpus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A publicly available corpus for training and evaluating supervised machine learning algorithms was created.
alignments.rationale: Both relate to the creation/use of a public corpus and subsequent analysis; the publication states the corpus creation explicitly while the preprint emphasized the error analysis enabled by it.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic classification can be used to identify additional users for potential inclusion in the cohort.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion (using automatic classification to find additional cohort members) does not appear in the preprint and is introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical detection approach outperforms the deep learning based methods in the majority of the datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The semisupervised method, based on Gaussian Mixture Models, outperforms the deep learning‐based methods in the majority of the data sets for fruit detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication names and specifies the classical approach as a semisupervised GMM method but preserves the same comparative result across datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning based approach performs better for fruit counting across all datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning‐based approach performs better for all of the data sets in fruit counting.
alignments.rationale: The claim about deep-learning superiority for counting is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining the classical detection method with the neural network based counting approach achieves yield accuracies ranging from 95.56% to 97.83%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining the semisupervised method and the deep learning‐based approach achieves yield estimation accuracies ranging from 95.56% to 97.83%.
alignments.rationale: The combined-method accuracy figures are identical; the publication specifies the classical method as semisupervised GMM but otherwise retains the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first work comparing multiple fruit detection and counting methods head‐to‐head on the same data sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts novelty (first head-to-head comparison) which was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The skin cancer predictions from content based image retrieval (CBIR) showed AUC values similar to softmax predictions across three datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Skin cancer predictions from content-based image retrieval (CBIR) showed AUC values similar to softmax predictions across three datasets.
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced verbatim; no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multiclass-accuracy of CBIR was comparable to softmax predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multiclass-accuracy of CBIR was comparable with softmax predictions.
alignments.rationale: The multiclass-accuracy comparison is stated equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks trained for detecting only 3 classes performed better on a dataset with 8 classes when using CBIR compared to softmax predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks trained for detecting only three classes performed better on a dataset with eight classes when using CBIR compared to softmax predictions.
alignments.rationale: The core result is the same, but the publication lowered the stated certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Presenting visually similar images based on features from a neural network shows comparable accuracy to the softmax probability-based diagnoses of convolutional neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Presenting visually similar images based on features from a neural network shows comparable accuracy with softmax probability-based diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about comparable accuracy when presenting similar images is consistent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CBIR may be more helpful than a softmax classifier in improving diagnostic accuracy of clinicians in a routine clinical setting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CBIR may be more helpful than a softmax classifier in improving diagnostic accuracy of clinicians in a routine clinical setting.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same cautious conclusion about potential clinical utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can simultaneously detect the lateral and axial positions, and the particle sizes via a deep neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can simultaneously detect the lateral and axial positions, and the particle sizes via a deep neural network.
alignments.rationale: The main technical capability is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep neural network demonstrates improved performance in detecting positions and sizes compared to conventional methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report a general performance improvement versus conventional methods; instead it introduces a specific optical-resolution conclusion not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The calculation time of the proposed method is faster than conventional diffracted-based approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The calculation time of the proposed method is faster than conventional diffracted-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The statement about faster calculation time is retained verbatim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The axial resolution of the proposed method is not limited by the numerical aperture of the optical system.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new specific optical conclusion (axial resolution not limited by NA) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel Deep Learning model for light curve classification captures essential classification patterns regardless of cadence and filter.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel deep-learning model for light-curve classification captures essential classification patterns regardless of cadence and filter.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates state of the art levels of accuracy in light curve classification benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model obtains state-of-the-art level accuracy in light-curve classification benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The claim of state-of-the-art accuracy is preserved with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a novel data augmentation schema for unevenly sampled time series enhances the model's performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel data augmentation schema for unevenly sampled time series is introduced in this work.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the introduction of the augmentation schema explicitly; the preprint emphasized its performance benefit. The core contribution is the same but the paper wording is more neutral about the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model addresses the challenges of integrating light curves from different surveys by eliminating the need for expensive data-calibration models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture allows for scalable machine-learning applications without the need for expensive integration techniques.
alignments.rationale: Both describe removing the need for costly integration/calibration; the publication reframes this as enabling scalable applications, clarifying the practical impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning framework for air-pollution monitoring and forecasting allows training across different model domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning framework for air-pollution monitoring and forecasting allows training across different model domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim text are identical in meaning and emphasis, so there is no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning framework achieves a reduction in run-time by two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning framework achieves a reduction in run-time by two orders of magnitude. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about run-time reduction is carried verbatim into the publication with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This implementation is the first to combine deep-learning and domain-decomposition techniques for model deployments beyond the trained domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This implementation is the first to combine deep-learning and domain-decomposition techniques for model deployments beyond the trained domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved exactly in the publication, so it is unchanged in semantics, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Near-future large galaxy surveys will encounter blended galaxy images at a fraction of up to 50% in the densest regions of the universe. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Near-future large galaxy surveys will encounter blended galaxy images at a fraction of up to 50 percent in the densest regions of the Universe. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same projection with equivalent wording and punctuation; the claim is effectively the same though label changed from conclusion to result, so classified as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel branched generative adversarial network (GAN) to deblend overlapping galaxies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel branched generative adversarial network to deblend overlapping galaxies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented verbatim in the publication with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generative models are a powerful engine for deblending given their innate ability to infill missing pixel values occluded by the superposition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative models are a powerful engine for deblending given their innate ability to infill missing pixel values occluded by the superposition. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The textual content is the same, but the publication frames it as a conclusion rather than a result; semantic content is preserved, so marked as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model maintains high peak signal-to-noise ratio and structural similarity scores with respect to ground truth images upon deblending. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model maintains high peak signal-to-noise ratio and structural similarity scores with respect to ground truth images upon deblending. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication in wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model predicts near-instantaneously, making it a natural choice for the immense quantities of data soon to be created by large surveys. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model predicts near-instantaneously, making it a natural choice for the immense quantities of data soon to be created by large surveys. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about prediction speed and suitability for large surveys is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of vision-based semantic segmentation models is significantly affected by variations in environmental conditions such as illumination and precipitation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of semantic segmentation models varies depending on the weather, camera parameters, and existence of shadows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the environmental sensitivity claim with slightly different phrasing and an explicit list of factors; content is consistent so treated as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method for analyzing the robustness of semantic segmentation models has been developed, which includes metrics for evaluating classification performance across various environmental conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel method for analyzing the robustness of semantic segmentation models is presented, which includes metrics to evaluate classification performance over various environmental conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim appears verbatim with equivalent meaning and scope in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that semantic segmentation performance varies based on factors like weather, camera parameters, and the presence of shadows. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of semantic segmentation models varies depending on the weather, camera parameters, and existence of shadows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental-result phrasing in the preprint maps to the publication's explicit statement of variation; the publication expresses this with higher certainty, so labeled as clarified with amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system allows for the validation of vision sensor performance against lidar data, which is essential for detecting inherent failures in vision technology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metrics developed can be used to compare and validate the performance of different networks and improvements made to a model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts focus from validating vision sensor performance against lidar specifically to using metrics to compare networks and validate model improvements; this narrows and softens the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An efficient method to detect leaf disease in crops has been proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient method to detect leaf disease in crops has been proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leaf diseases can be detected from sample images of the leaf using image processing and segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leaf diseases can be detected from simple images of the leaves using image processing and segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses nearly identical wording with 'simple images' instead of 'sample images'; the meaning is the same so treated as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-means clustering and Otsu's method are used to detect the faulty region in a leaf. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: K-means clustering and Otsu's method are effective for detecting the faulty region in a leaf. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the wording to state the methods are 'effective', but the underlying technical claim is the same, so classified as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Calculating the ratio of normal and faulty regions can predict if the leaf can be cured. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Calculating the ratio of normal and faulty regions can predict if a leaf can be cured. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The predictive-conclusion claim is unchanged in phrasing and confidence between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NestDNN achieves as much as a 4.2% increase in inference accuracy compared to the resource-agnostic status quo approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NestDNN achieves as much as 4.2% increase in inference accuracy compared to the resource-agnostic status quo approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The accuracy improvement claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NestDNN results in a 2.0x increase in video frame processing rate compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NestDNN results in a 2.0× increase in video frame processing rate compared to the resource-agnostic status quo approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparison baseline as the resource-agnostic status quo; the quantitative claim is otherwise unchanged, so treated as clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NestDNN reduces energy consumption by 1.7x compared to the resource-agnostic approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NestDNN leads to a 1.7× reduction in energy consumption compared to the resource-agnostic status quo approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same energy reduction with slightly adjusted wording; the substance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NestDNN enables flexible resource-accuracy trade-offs for deep learning models in mobile vision systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NestDNN enables flexible resource-accuracy trade-offs for deep learning models in mobile vision systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about enabling resource-accuracy trade-offs is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new algorithm for reconstructing the 2-D (x; y) position and the size of the charge signal (e) of an interaction is presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new algorithm for reconstructing the 2-D (x,y) position and the size of the charge signal (e) of an interaction is presented.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BOLFI improved the accuracy of the reconstruction over the next best algorithm by up to 15% for events over a large radii (R > 30 cm).
alignments.pub_claim_text: BOLFI improved the accuracy of the reconstruction over the next best algorithm by up to 15% when focusing on events at large radii (R > 30 cm).
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different phrasing but conveys the same quantitative claim and radius condition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BOLFI provides the smallest uncertainties among all the tested methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BOLFI provides the smallest uncertainties among all the tested methods.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and scope in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical model of sensorimotor coordination contributes to self-other distinction during action and perception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical model of sensorimotor coordination contributes to self-other distinction during action and perception.
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in wording and intent in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model minimizes free energy during perception and action.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is able to minimize free energy during perception and action.
alignments.rationale: Minor phrasing change only; the substantive claim is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can correctly attribute a sense of agency to one's own actions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can correctly attribute a sense of agency to own actions.
alignments.rationale: Same claim with trivial wording adjustment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We derive Bayesian approximate message passing (AMP) algorithms for recovering arbitrarily shaped low-rank tensors buried within noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian approximate message passing (AMP) algorithms can recover arbitrarily shaped low-rank tensors buried within noise.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the derived capability as a result but the technical content is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our theory reveals the existence of phase transitions between easy, hard and impossible inference regimes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical analysis reveals the existence of phase transitions between easy, hard, and impossible inference regimes.
alignments.rationale: Same conceptual claim; publication wording is stylistically different but not substantively changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our theory displays an excellent match with simulations.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about an 'excellent match with simulations' does not appear among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AMP significantly outperforms ALS in the presence of noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the AMP algorithm significantly outperforms the alternating least squares (ALS) algorithm in the presence of noise.
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative performance comparison present in both versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavior of low-rank tensor decomposition shows several qualitative surprises compared to symmetric, cubic tensor decomposition.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims; it is an added observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm outperforms standard grid scan techniques, reducing the number of measurements required by up to 4 times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm outperforms standard grid scan techniques, reducing the number of measurements required by up to 4 times.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm reduces the measurement time by 3.7 times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The measurement time is reduced by 3.7 times when using the machine learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative reduction claim with minor phrasing change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work lays the foundation for automated control of large quantum circuits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work lays the foundation for learning-based automated measurement of quantum devices.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from 'automated control of large quantum circuits' to 'learning-based automated measurement of quantum devices', which narrows and revises the original claimed scope and reduces stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepLSR reduces laser speckle noise by 6.4 dB in images of gastrointestinal tissues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepLSR reduces laser speckle noise by 6.4 dB in images of gastrointestinal tissues.
alignments.rationale: Exact match between preprint and publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepLSR outperforms optimized non-local means processing, BM3D, and an optical speckle reducer, achieving greater noise reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepLSR outperforms optimized non-local means processing, BM3D, and an optical speckle reducer, achieving greater noise reduction.
alignments.rationale: Identical comparative performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining DeepLSR with optical speckle reduction can achieve a total speckle noise reduction of 9.4 dB.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining DeepLSR with optical speckle reduction can achieve a total speckle noise reduction of 9.4 dB.
alignments.rationale: Quantitative combination claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reduction in speckle noise may enable the use of coherent light sources in high-quality imaging applications, such as medical endoscopy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reduction in speckle noise may enable the use of coherent light sources in high-quality imaging applications, such as medical endoscopy.
alignments.rationale: Same forward-looking conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adversaries can craft adversarial examples that cause neural networks to misclassify inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversaries can craft adversarial examples for neural networks that cause misclassification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stochastic substitute training allows adversaries to craft adversarial examples for defenses that obfuscate gradients without knowledge of the defense. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stochastic substitute training allows adversaries to craft adversarial examples without knowledge of the defense. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim preserves the core claim (crafting attacks without defense knowledge) with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With stochastic substitute training, an adversary only needs limited information about the defense to craft adversarial examples against detection mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With limited information about the defense, adversaries can still craft adversarial examples against detection mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about limited-information attacks against detectors with the same meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique was demonstrated against two defenses that enhance model robustness and two defenses that detect adversarial examples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The technique was demonstrated against two defenses that enhance model robustness and two that detect adversarial examples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Demonstration details are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) drastically reduce the energy consumption of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) by encoding information in sparse temporal binary spike streams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) drastically reduce the energy consumption of Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) by encoding information in sparse temporal binary spike streams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical result with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNNs are important candidates as co-processors to be implemented in mobile devices due to their low energy consumption. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNNs are important candidates as co-processors to be implemented in mobile devices due to their low energy consumption. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the capability of online trained SNNs as stochastic policies to gracefully trade energy consumption and control performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Online trained SNNs as stochastic policies can gracefully trade energy consumption and control performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim conveys the same experimental finding with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant gains are shown when comparing the performance of online trained SNNs to the standard approach of converting an offline trained ANN into an SNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant gains are shown when comparing the performance of online trained SNNs to the standard approach of converting an offline trained ANN into an SNN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparison result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks are generally insensitive to the precision of the activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks are generally insensitive to the precision of the activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim and meaning in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed combinational circuit-based approach is very efficient in terms of speed and area, with negligible accuracy loss on the MNIST, CIFAR-10 and IMAGENET benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed combinational circuit-based approach is very efficient in terms of speed and area, with negligible accuracy loss on the MNIST, CIFAR-10, and IMAGE NET benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Minor formatting differences in benchmark naming only; substantive claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Circuit designs for tanh and SELU can save between 3.13-7.69 and 4.45-8.45 area compared to the LUT/memory-based implementations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Circuit designs for tanh and SELU can save between ${\times 3.13\text{ to } \times 7.69}$ and ${ {\times 4.45\text{ to } \times 8.45}}$ area compared to the look-up table/memory-based implementations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Mathematical/formatting differences in the publication aside, the numeric area-savings claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The circuit designs for tanh and SELU can operate at 5.14GHz and 4.52GHz using the 28nm SVT library, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The circuit designs for tanh and SELU can operate at 5.14 GHz and 4.52 GHz using the 28-nm SVT library, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Operational frequency claims are identical aside from typographical spacing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNNs have a significant advantage over shallow networks when the experimental dataset used for preparing models is small. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNNs have a significant advantage over shallow networks when the experimental dataset used for preparing models is small. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core finding is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal number of layers in DNNs varies between 5 to 7. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal number of layers in DNNs varies between 5 to 7, with networks having less or more layers obtaining lower accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication merges this with the adjacent claim and presents it more strongly (higher stated certainty) and more explicitly linking layer count to accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks with less and more layers obtain lower accuracy compared to those with the optimal number of layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal number of layers in DNNs varies between 5 to 7, with networks having less or more layers obtaining lower accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines this claim with the optimal-layer statement; content is preserved but consolidated into a single clearer claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of neurons in DNN has a lower impact on the prediction accuracy than the number of DNN's layers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of neurons in DNN has a lower impact on the prediction accuracy than the number of DNN’s layers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published wording preserves the relationship but downgrades the certainty and frames it more as a conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Obtaining small but high-quality experimental training datasets is advisable for predicting the ultimate bearing capacity with ANN models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Obtaining small but high-quality experimental training datasets is advisable for predicting the ultimate bearing capacity with ANN models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is repeated without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DNNs perform very well, even when trained with only 6 samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about performance with only 6 samples does not appear in the preprint and is newly present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for automatic ColorChecker detection is fast and robust to overlaps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for automatic ColorChecker detection is fast and robust to overlaps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates invariance to affine projections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is invariant to affine projections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same technical property asserted in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm performs well in the detection of multiple ColorCheckers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm performs well in the case of multiple ColorCheckers detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance on multiple ColorCheckers is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dropout at each feed-forward connection severely affects the model accuracy at increasing dropout rates. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dropout at each feed-forward connection severely affects the model accuracy at increasing dropout rates. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually identical and convey the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regularizing the embedding layer is efficient for the SNLI dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Regularizing the embedding layer is efficient for the SNLI dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in content and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regularizing the recurrent layer improves the accuracy for the SciTail dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Regularizing the recurrent layer improves the accuracy for the SciTail dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Direct one-to-one match with identical wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed RNN model achieved an accuracy of 86.14% on the SNLI dataset and 77.05% on SciTail. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RNN model achieved an accuracy of 86.14% on the SNLI dataset and 77.05% on the SciTail dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric result is the same; publication only adds the word 'dataset' for SciTail, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We enable material-aware 3D shape analysis by employing a projective convolutional neural network architecture. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We enable material-aware 3D shape analysis by employing a projective convolutional neural network architecture. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of contribution and wording between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We crowdsource a dataset of 3080 3D shapes with part-wise material labels to address the challenge of limited labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We crowdsource a dataset of 3080 3D shapes with part-wise material labels to address the challenge of limited labeled data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset size and role are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the effectiveness of our learned descriptors for automatic texturing, material-aware retrieval, and physical simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the effectiveness of our learned descriptors for automatic texturing, material-aware retrieval, and physical simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental results and listed applications are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We contribute a high-quality expert-labeled benchmark of 115 shapes from Herman-Miller and IKEA for evaluation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We contribute a high-quality expert-labeled benchmark of 115 shapes from Herman-Miller and IKEA for evaluation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The benchmark contribution is present with identical details in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for audio-based activity recognition achieves 64.2% and 83.6% averaged within-subject accuracy for top-1 and top-3 classification respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our framework achieves 64.2% and 83.6% averaged within-subject accuracy for top-1 and top-3 classification of 15 everyday activities respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the numeric results and explicitly ties them to the 15-activity task, adding clarifying specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework utilizes millions of embedding features from public online video sound clips without the need for further feature processing or outliers filtering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework for audio-based activity recognition can utilize millions of embedding features from public online video sound clips. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes the ability to utilize millions of embeddings but separates the 'no further processing' point (presented elsewhere), so the original combined claim is split and rephrased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study evaluates the recognition of 15 everyday activities in the context of Activities of Daily Living (ADL) with 14 participants in their own homes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate participant count or in-home evaluation details; the experimental context detail from the preprint is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual class performance was examined to study the co-occurrence characteristics of the activities and the robustness of the framework. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a standalone claim about examining individual-class co-occurrence and robustness; that preprint conclusion is not explicitly present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework does not require further feature processing or outliers filtering, unlike prior work. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the 'no further processing' point as a separate, emphasized claim; while the preprint mentioned it within another claim, the publication treats it as a standalone statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Molecule Deep Q-Networks (MolDQN) framework ensures 100% chemical validity in molecule modifications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecule Deep Q-Networks (MolDQN) ensures 100% chemical validity in molecule modifications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The chemical validity guarantee is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MolDQN operates without pre-training on any dataset to avoid bias. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about lack of pre-training to avoid bias; that specific operational claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model extends to multi-objective reinforcement learning to maximize drug-likeness while maintaining similarity to the original molecule. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is extended with multi-objective reinforcement learning to maximize drug-likeness while maintaining similarity to the original molecule. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The multi-objective extension is present with the same goals and phrasing in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provides a path through chemical space for molecule optimization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The path through chemical space to achieve optimization for a molecule is shown to understand how the model works. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the path-through-chemical-space result but clarifies its purpose (to understand model behavior).

alignments.pub_claim_text: MolDQN achieves comparable or better performance against several other recently published algorithms for benchmark molecular optimization tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit comparative-performance claim that was not asserted in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Many benchmark molecular optimization tasks are not representative of real optimization problems in drug discovery. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a limitation/critique about benchmarks that is not present as a preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hitting nails is more central to people's concept of a hammer than its particular material composition or other intrinsic features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human concepts are primarily organized around goals and interactions rather than their intrinsic features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the hammer example into a broader conclusion about conceptual organization; the core claim about goals vs intrinsic features is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using product co-occurrence data from nearly 1.3m shopping baskets, we trained a topic model to learn 25 high-level concepts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of methods devised for text to product co-occurrence data reveals 25 high-level concepts that are comprehensible and coherent to both retail experts and consumers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication merges the dataset/method statement with evaluation of concept quality; it omits the exact basket count but preserves the core result and adds assessment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Topics were found to be comprehensible and coherent by both retail experts and consumers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of methods devised for text to product co-occurrence data reveals 25 high-level concepts that are comprehensible and coherent to both retail experts and consumers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint claim is directly present in the publication text (the publication bundles it with the methodological claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual differences in the topics sampled predicted basic demographic characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in the topics sampled can predict basic demographic characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictive relationship and certainty are preserved with only minor wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results suggest that human activity patterns reveal conceptual organization and may give rise to it. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human activity patterns can reveal and may give rise to conceptual organization. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The tentative conclusion is retained with equivalent meaning in the publication.
